formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Pascal's Law",
    "formula": "Pressure applied at any point in a confined fluid is transmitted undiminished in all directions throughout the fluid.",
    "notes": "This law explains how pressure is transmitted in a fluid. It's essential for understanding fluid dynamics and pressure in fluids."
  },
  {
    "name": "Archimedes' Principle",
    "formula": "The buoyant force on an object submerged in a fluid is equal to the weight of the fluid displaced by the object.",
    "variables": "Fb = weight of fluid displaced, m = mass of fluid displaced, V = volume of fluid displaced, ρ = density of fluid, g = acceleration due to gravity.",
    "units": "Fb in Newtons (N), m in kg, V in cubic meters (m³), ρ in kg/m³, g in m/s².",
    "notes": "This principle helps calculate the buoyant force on an object when submerged in a fluid."
  },
  {
    "name": "Bernoulli's Equation",
    "formula": "P + (1/2)ρv² + ρgh = constant",
    "variables": "P = pressure of fluid, ρ = density of fluid, v = velocity of fluid, g = acceleration due to gravity, h = height.",
    "units": "P in Pascals (Pa), ρ in kg/m³, v in m/s, g in m/s², h in meters (m).",
    "notes": "This equation relates the pressure and velocity of a fluid in motion. It's essential for understanding fluid dynamics and pressure in fluids."
  },
  {
    "name": "Pressure Formula",
    "formula": "P = F/A",
    "variables": "P = pressure of fluid, F = force exerted by fluid, A = area of the surface.",
    "units": "P in Pascals (Pa), F in Newtons (N), A in square meters (m²).",
    "notes": "This formula calculates the pressure exerted by a fluid on a surface."
  },
  {
    "name": "Viscosity Formula",
    "formula": "η = τ/du/dy",
    "variables": "η = dynamic viscosity, τ = shear stress, du/dy = shear rate.",
    "units": "η in Pa·s, τ in Pascals (Pa), du/dy in s⁻¹.",
    "notes": "This formula calculates the dynamic viscosity of a fluid, which is a measure of its resistance to shear stress."
  },
  {
    "name": "Poiseuille's Law",
    "formula": "Q = (πr⁴ΔP)/(8ηl)",
    "variables": "Q = volume flow rate, r = radius of pipe, ΔP = pressure difference, η = dynamic viscosity, l = length of pipe.",
    "units": "Q in m³/s, r in meters (m), ΔP in Pascals (Pa), η in Pa·s, l in meters (m).",
    "notes": "This law calculates the volume flow rate of a fluid through a cylindrical pipe."
  },
  {
    "name": "Torricelli's Theorem",
    "formula": "v = √(2gh)",
    "variables": "v = velocity of fluid, g = acceleration due to gravity, h = height.",
    "units": "v in m/s, g in m/s², h in meters (m).",
    "notes": "This theorem calculates the velocity of a fluid when it flows out of a small opening at the bottom of a container."
  },
  {
    "name": "Co-efficient of Viscosity Formula",
    "formula": "η = μ / ρ",
    "variables": "η = co-efficient of viscosity, μ = dynamic viscosity, ρ = density of fluid.",
    "units": "η in m²/s, μ in Pa·s, ρ in kg/m³.",
    "notes": "This formula calculates the co-efficient of viscosity, which is a measure of a fluid's resistance to shear stress."
  },
  {
    "name": "Surface Tension Formula",
    "formula": "γ = F/A",
    "variables": "γ = surface tension, F = force exerted by surface tension, A = area of the surface.",
    "units": "γ in N/m, F in Newtons (N), A in square meters (m²).",
    "notes": "This formula calculates the surface tension of a fluid, which is a measure of its energy per unit area."
  }
]
```

This JSON array contains the most relevant formulas and laws for the Mechanical Properties of Fluids chapter in CBSE Class 11 Physics. It includes formulas for pressure, viscosity, and surface tension, as well as laws like Pascal's Law, Archimedes' Principle, and Bernoulli's Equation.
